implementation_notes:
Three surgical edits only — no other lines touched.

Issue 1 (pipeline_consumer.py ~line 365): Wrapped the bare `await msg.ack()` on the duplicate-skip path in a try/except Exception block that logs a WARNING with subject and exception, then continues (return is outside the try block so it still fires). CancelledError is not caught because it inherits from BaseException, not Exception.

Issue 2 (pipeline_consumer.py ~line 519 after edit): Wrapped the bare `await msg.ack()` at the end of `_reject` in the same try/except Exception pattern with a WARNING log. This is independent of the existing try/except around the publish call above it.

Issue 3 (pyproject.toml line 18): Added `nats-py>=2.7,<3` as an explicit entry in [project.dependencies], immediately after the existing `nats-core` line. The existing `nats-core` entry was left in place.
requirements_addressed: Unguarded msg.ack() on duplicate-skip path wrapped in try/except Exception with WARNING log
Unguarded msg.ack() inside _reject wrapped in try/except Exception with WARNING log
nats-py declared as direct dependency in pyproject.toml [project.dependencies] with version spec nats-py>=2.7,<3
